title: Los Pumas Make Five Changes for Nations Championship Clash Against Wales
summary: Los Pumas Make Five Changes for Nations Championship Clash Against Wales

fullArticle:
After suffering a 47-38 defeat to Scotland in the opening match of the inaugural Nations Championship in Córdoba, Los Pumas will look to bounce back when they host Wales at Estadio San Juan del Bicentenario on Saturday.

The fixture will be Argentina's eighth official Test match in San Juan and the second time they have faced Wales at the venue. The sides previously met there in 2018, when the visitors claimed a 23-10 victory.

Head coach Felipe Contepomi has made five changes to the starting XV from the side that faced Scotland last weekend. Boris Wenger comes in for Mayco Vivas, Tomás Rapetti replaces Pedro Delgado, Marcos Kremer returns in place of Pablo Matera, Justo Piccardo starts instead of Faustino Sánchez Valarolo, and Bautista Delguy replaces Rodrigo Isgró on the wing.
